key_departures.impact: Robinson was the Giants' starting slot receiver and came off a career year in 2025 with 92 catches for 1,014 yards and 4 TDs. He was the primary target for Jaxson Dart's development. Giants did not use the franchise tag. Departure leaves a significant void at slot WR.

Flott started 14 games in 2025 and posted career highs with 11 pass deflections and 1 INT. Giants reportedly believed they had a deal with Flott before he chose Tennessee. The loss significantly hurts an already-thin secondary and was not anticipated this cleanly.

Giants released Okereke to save $9M in cap space. He finished 2025 with 143 tackles (4th-most in franchise history) and was the Walter Payton Man of the Year club winner. Cut was cap-driven ahead of free agency to fund upgrades. Creates a starter-level hole at ILB.

Bellinger's role had shrunk to 19 catches in 2025 with the emergence of Theo Johnson, and his departure was expected once the Giants signed Isaiah Likely. Minimal impact on needs given TE room is now strengthened.
Belton was the Giants' special teams tackle leader but never carved out a full-time starting role. Departure is a depth loss at safety and special teams.

Schlottmann was a versatile backup who could play center and guard. His departure leaves the Giants without a backup center under contract, an underrated hole given John Michael Schmitz is the only true center on the roster.
Cap casualty. Gano missed all of 2025 with a hip injury. Cut saves approximately $4.5M. Team is transitioning to Harbaugh-era kicking staff.
Cut as part of Harbaugh's specialist overhaul. Gillan was considered inconsistent and has been directly replaced by Jordan Stout.
Cut to save approximately $5.38M. Hudson had been passed on the depth chart by Marcus Mbow. Minimal need impact as he was not a starter.

signings.analysis: Eluemunor re-signs at a below-market rate ($13M AAV vs. a potential $18-20M market) after posting a 98% PFF pass-blocking efficiency rating in 2025. Three years, $26M guaranteed is a real commitment — this is their right tackle for the foreseeable future. Retaining him as the bookend opposite Andrew Thomas preserves an OL that ranked 4th in PFF pass-blocking efficiency last season. OT need is fully resolved. Giants can now target IOL in the draft instead.

Giants released Bobby Okereke (9M savings) and immediately pivoted to Edmunds, a younger, more athletic option at 27. Edmunds has topped 100 tackles in each of his 8 NFL seasons and has 14 career INTs. At 3yr/$36M with $23.7M guaranteed, this is a genuine starter-level commitment — not a bridge signing. He replaces the green-dot role vacated by Okereke and fits the sideline-to-sideline athleticism Harbaugh's defense demands. LB need created by Okereke's release is fully addressed.

Likely (3yr/$40M, max $47.5M) is a scheme-defining acquisition for Harbaugh's offense. He'll line up as the move TE/'F' tight end and is expected to slot into Robinson's vacated pass-catcher role in 12 personnel heavy sets alongside Theo Johnson. His 3yr commitment signals this is a starter investment, not a bridge. However, he is a TE, not a true WR replacement — the slot WR position remains an open question. ESPN noted the Giants may still pursue a WR in later waves. Guarantee structure not yet fully confirmed per Spotrac; AAV ranks 6th at TE. Need at WR partially addressed schematically but true slot WR remains a need.

Giants pivoted to Newsome after losing Flott to the Titans. The deal (1yr/$8M, max $10M, only $3M guaranteed) is structured as a prove-it/bridge contract with minimal financial commitment. Newsome has starting experience (58 career starts) and is only 25, but his play declined sharply in 2024-25 and he was traded mid-season by Cleveland. One year and $3M in guarantees is a low-commitment stopgap, not a solution. CB remains a significant draft need — this does not resolve the Flott hole.

Ricard (2yr/$7.63M base, max $8.5M) is the highest-paid fullback in the NFL and the highest-profile 'Harbaugh Raven' addition. Six-time Pro Bowler and first-team All-Pro in 2025. Fullback was not a pre-existing need (Giants had no FB before this). This is a scheme-creation move — Harbaugh is building a power-run identity around Ricard leading Cam Skattebo. Guarantee structure not confirmed per Spotrac. Does not address any pre-existing positional need but elevates the offense conceptually.

Stout becomes the NFL's highest-paid punter, replacing the released Jamie Gillan. He was a first-team All-Pro in 2025, averaging 50.1 yards per punt. Three-year deal is a genuine investment in the specialist unit, a stated priority for Harbaugh. Specialist need fully addressed.

Washington replaces Dane Belton as the depth/versatile DB behind Jevon Holland. He is coming off a torn Achilles that cost him most of 2025. One-year deal at $3M is depth-level investment. He brings Baltimore system familiarity and can play slot, free safety, and box. Minimal guarantee structure means this is a camp competition signing more than a starter solution. S need unchanged at depth level.

Hodgins re-signs on a low-cost one-year deal after rejoining the Giants mid-2025. Made five starts last season. He is a physically imposing blocker who fits Harbaugh's scheme on the boundary. However, this does not solve the slot WR vacancy left by Robinson. WR need remains elevated. Exact terms not yet confirmed.

Olszewski re-signs on a one-year deal as a return specialist and slot depth option. He filled in adequately for Robinson in the slot when needed in 2025. Will compete for the slot role in camp but is a special teams asset first. Does not resolve WR need. Exact terms not yet confirmed.

Manhertz re-signs on a low-cost deal as the team's primary blocking TE behind Likely and Theo Johnson. His in-line blocking was integral to the run game. One-year deal maintains familiar depth. No positional need impact — TE room is now well-stocked.

Sanders (former All-Pro kicker) signs a one-year deal but missed all of 2025 due to a hip injury — his signing is contingent on passing a physical. He will compete with Ben Sauls, who made all 8 FG attempts as a rookie. One-year deal with health uncertainty means K need is partially addressed, not resolved. Giants have Sauls as insurance.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Eluemunor re-signed on a 3yr/$39M deal ($26M gtd). Bookend tackles (Thomas + Eluemunor) are locked in. Marcus Mbow is a capable swing tackle in development. OT is no longer a need — remove from board entirely.

Edmunds (3yr/$36M, $23.7M gtd) fully replaces Okereke with a younger, more athletic option. The LB need created by Okereke's cut is addressed. Giants may still want a developmental Day 3 LB but it is not a priority. Tier drops significantly.

Robinson's departure (4yr/$78M to Titans) escalates WR from a moderate need to the team's most pressing positional gap. Likely partially fills the pass-catcher void schematically as a move TE, but a true slot WR has not been signed. Hodgins and Olszewski are not starter-caliber replacements. WR (especially slot) should be a top-3 draft or FA priority.

Flott departure (3yr/$45M to Titans) removes the best CB2 the Giants had. Newsome's 1yr/$8M ($3M gtd) deal is a bridge/prove-it signing, not a solution. Giants missed on multiple other CBs (Jamel Dean to Steelers). CB opposite Paulson Adebo is now a top draft priority — this position may be the most urgent on the entire board.

Schlottmann's departure (to Titans) removes the team's only backup center. Greg Van Roten and Jon Runyan Jr. statuses remain uncertain. The Giants missed on guard Alijah Vera-Tucker (went to Patriots). Interior line (C depth, RG) remains a draft and/or late FA priority. Need unchanged — may escalate further if Van Roten or Runyan depart.
Likely (3yr/$40M) joins Theo Johnson and re-signed Manhertz. Giants will deploy heavy 12 personnel. TE is now a position of strength; remove from need board.

Belton's departure to Jets is a depth loss, replaced at that tier by Washington (1yr/$3M). Jevon Holland remains the starter. Need at S is unchanged — still a Day 3 draft or mid-FA target for a developmental piece.

No EDGE additions or departures of note in FA. Brian Burns remains on the roster. Kayvon Thibodeaux's status is uncertain (trade candidate per multiple reports). EDGE need unchanged — monitor Thibodeaux situation closely.

key_additions.headline: 100+ tackle machine replaces cut Bobby Okereke as the green-dot leader of Harbaugh's defense
Harbaugh reunites with his Ravens TE to be Dart's top weapon in a 12-personnel-heavy offense
Top-10 pass-blocking RT retained at below-market rate, preserving the bookend duo protecting Jaxson Dart
First-round pedigree CB fills the Flott void on a prove-it deal, but CB remains a critical draft need
6x Pro Bowl fullback and 3x All-Pro arrives to anchor Harbaugh's power-run identity alongside Cam Skattebo
All-Pro punter (50.1 yds/punt in 2025) becomes NFL's highest-paid punter as Harbaugh overhauls the specialist unit
Versatile Ravens DB replaces Belton as depth safety and special teams contributor in Harbaugh's system

key_losses.headline: Career-year slot receiver (92/1014/4 in 2025) departs on a massive deal, leaving the biggest offensive hole on the board
Emerging CB2 (11 PDs, 1 INT in 2025) follows Daboll to Tennessee, escalating CB to a top-2 draft priority
143-tackle franchise icon cut to create cap space; immediately replaced by Tremaine Edmunds
Popular blocking TE follows Daboll to Tennessee; departure expected after Isaiah Likely signing
Versatile C/G backup departs, leaving Giants without a backup center under contract

notes: Contract guarantees for Isaiah Likely not yet fully confirmed per Spotrac as of March 11 (base value $40M confirmed; max value $47.5M per multiple reports). Patrick Ricard guarantee structure not yet confirmed. Isaiah Hodgins and Gunner Olszewski exact contract terms not yet reported. Emmanuel Sanders contract terms not yet fully disclosed. Tremaine Edmunds deal confirmed at 3yr/$36M/$23.7M guaranteed per NFL Network/ESPN. All other contract data sourced from Spotrac, OverTheCap, ESPN, and Adam Schefter/Ian Rapoport reports.
